definition: Grimoire splices or replicates generalized token substructures multiple times within a single input, accumulating enough instances (attribute declarations, content-model children, hash entries, duplicate element decls) to push an internal counter or structural condition past a threshold that triggers an otherwise-unreached branch.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural mutators splice generalized DTD substructures to replicate ATTLIST declarations past the initial maxAttrs of 4, triggering the realloc branch that byte-level havoc cannot reach by accumulating enough attribute declarations.
signature.one_line: Grimoire splices DTD attribute declarations past maxAttrs to trigger realloc branch

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must declare more than 4 default attributes for one element so nbAttrs reaches maxAttrs=4 and the realloc grow-branch fires.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD whose ATTLIST declares >4 default-valued attributes for a single element, so xmlAddDefAttrs is re-entered enough times for the same (name,prefix) entry that defaults->nbAttrs reaches the initial maxAttrs of 4 and the true (grow/realloc) side at line 1243 is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire-discovered winner seeds carry a compact, repeatable DTD structure (a small <!DOCTYPE ... SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"> shell) and Grimoire's structural mutators recursively splice/extend generalized token runs, letting it replicate the attribute-declaration substructure many times; the winner re-enters xmlAddDefAttrs far more often (entry-line 189 vs 60) and takes the blocker true side 30 times while the loser never does.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog mutates bytes (havoc + I2S substitution) but cannot synthesize a single element accumulating 5+ default-attribute declarations; its larger 368-377 byte seeds spend executions elsewhere and the blocker true side stays at T=0, so defaults->nbAttrs never reaches maxAttrs.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire share the same input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so that technique cannot be the differentiator; the single-technique delta is grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural/grammar-free recombination mutators, which repeat the generalized attribute-declaration substructure enough times to push nbAttrs past maxAttrs.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side at parser.c:1243 grows the defaults array only when nbAttrs has reached maxAttrs.
maxAttrs is initialized to 4 on first allocation, so the grow branch needs more than 4 declarations for the same element.
Taking the true side doubles maxAttrs via realloc, confirming the grow semantics of the winning branch.
The winner re-enters xmlAddDefAttrs far more often than the loser (entry-line proxy 189 vs 60).
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side 30 times while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1243  T=30 F=111  T=0 F=30  } else if (defaults->nbAttrs >= defaults->maxAttrs) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds are grimoire-discovered with a compact repeated DTD shell.
Loser seeds are cmplog-discovered and larger.
Both cmplog and grimoire include I2SRandReplace, so the single-technique delta is grimoire's generalization plus structural mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire mutators splice and recurse on generalized token/gap structures to perform structural recombination.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the blocker fires after a single element accumulates >4 default attributes, yet cmplog reaches the realloc true-side as often as grimoire (i.e. grimoire's structural repetition provides no advantage over plain havoc/I2S in driving nbAttrs past maxAttrs).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The winning seed bytes show a compact DTD shell but do not visibly contain 5+ attribute declarations in the 64-byte dump, so the count-of-declarations claim rests on hit-count divergence rather than directly observed declaration bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural Grimoire mutators grow the DTD content model past two children by recombining generalized token/gap structures, triggering the separator-loop re-entry that cmplog's byte-level havoc rarely reaches.
signature.one_line: Grimoire grows DTD content model past two children to trigger separator-loop re-entry

analysis.summary_one_line: A multi-element DTD content model whose separator loop runs twice then hits a non-separator/non-close token after a second child is parsed.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same deep multi-child content model, but reached by splicing/recombining generalised XML/DTD token structures rather than by gradient climbing; the input must still parse two children and re-enter the separator loop on an invalid token.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolved 8/10 with avg hitcount 12 on the true side; the winning seed is a well-formed '<?xml version="1.0"?>\n<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1' skeleton, and GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators recombine such generalised token runs to grow valid nested content models far past where byte-level havoc stalls.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolved only 2/10 and blocked 8/10; its mutators (havoc/token + I2S) mutate at the byte level without generalised structural recombination, so it rarely reproduces a content model deep enough to parse a second child and re-enter the separator loop.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The single-technique delta grimoire vs cmplog (both have I2S) is grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural Grimoire mutators: string-based grammar-free recombination of generalised token/gap structures builds the deep DTD content model; cmplog lacks this structural stage, so the structural recombination, not I2S, is the cause.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the error arm of the content-model separator loop, taken when the next token is neither ',' nor '|' nor ')'.
Taking the true side requires `last` to be non-NULL, i.e. a second child must have been parsed.
`last` is set when a second element child is parsed in the loop body.
value_profile_cmplog resolves this branch 10/10 while cmplog blocks 8/10, with the winner hitting the true side and the loser never doing so.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=6  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The single-technique delta for pair 1 is value_profile (CMP_MAP gradient on top of cmplog).
value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets over the cmplog stack.
The single-technique delta for pair 2 is grimoire_structural, and grimoire adds generalization plus Grimoire mutators on top of cmplog which already has I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators recombine generalised token structures grammar-free.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire resolves the branch 8/10 with a higher true-side hitcount than the loser, while cmplog blocks 8/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=12  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The grimoire winning seed is a well-formed XML/DOCTYPE skeleton suitable for structural recombination into deeper content models.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a single-element content model (no second child, so `last` stays NULL and line 6461 true-side is unreachable) showing value_profile_cmplog and grimoire still beating cmplog would refute the deep-multi-child-structure requirement.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff section is dominated by the shared XML header and shows only PARTIAL single-count W vs L differences, so the deep content-model structural divergence is inferred from source/coverage rather than directly visible in the shown seed bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural recombination splices repeated/recursive token structures that hash-collide on the same key slot, reaching the populated-slot else-branch that cmplog's linear havoc mutations never exercise.
signature.one_line: Grimoire splices repeated tokens causing hash collision to reach populated-slot branch

analysis.summary_one_line: Take the false branch by forcing a hash-slot collision: insert two DTD entries whose keys collide so table[key].valid is already 1.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that drives xmlHashUpdateEntry3 to a key slot that is already populated (valid != 0), reaching the else-branch insertion-chain walk at lines 687-728. This requires a second DTD/attribute structure that hash-collides with a prior entry, i.e. structurally repeated/recursive markup, not a single linear declaration.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire-produced winner seeds are compact (219-235 bytes) and carry a repeated/spliced token structure (the '<?l ?>' fragment plus the duplicated 'dtds/127772.dtd' run appearing twice in the dump), reflecting grimoire's structural recombination of generalized gaps; these reach the else-branch (W-only coverage at lines 687-728) and hit the branch 76 times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds are larger linear DTDs (368-377 bytes) with a single well-formed '<?xml version="1.0"?>' prolog; they only ever take the true branch (L 684 T=30 F=0, insert=NULL) and never enter the else-chain, so they never produce a colliding second entry that flips valid==0 to false.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers run input-to-state comparison-solving, so the single-technique delta is grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural Grimoire mutators (string/recursive replacement and extension). Grimoire splices generalized token/gap structures to synthesize repeated/recursive declarations that collide on the same hash key, exercising the populated-slot else-branch that cmplog's linear comparison-solving and havoc mutations do not assemble.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the false branch of the slot-occupancy test in xmlHashUpdateEntry3.
Globally the false branch is the blocked side.
Only the winner reaches the else-branch insertion-chain walk after an occupied slot.
The loser only ever takes the true branch at 684 (false count zero), never reaching the populated-slot path.

evidence_trail.claim: Loser code never enters the dict else-branch at line 687.

Winner hits the branch 76 times while loser hits it zero times.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural mutators build deeply nested multi-alternative DTD content models ('(a|b|...)') that create a parent->c2 sibling node; cmplog lacks structural recombination and never assembles such a model.
signature.one_line: Grimoire builds nested DTD content models with c2 sibling cmplog cannot reach

analysis.summary_one_line: A DTD <!ELEMENT> content model with a multi-child SEQ/OR group so the freed node is parent->c2, taking the false branch

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program condition: a DTD <!ELEMENT> content model with a multi-child SEQ/OR group whose freed node maps to parent->c2, but reached via structural recombination of generalised DTD token fragments rather than gradient byte search.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves the false branch in 8/10 trials with the highest hitcount on the branch (63), and its GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators splice generalised token/gap structures, letting it recombine '<!ELEMENT name (a|b|...)' fragments into deeply nested multi-alternative content models that create the c2 sibling driving line 1128.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline grimoire builds on) blocks 9/10 with hitcount 0; it lacks the generalization + structural recombination, so it cannot assemble the multi-child group and never produces a parent->c2 node to free.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators (string-based, grammar-free recombination of generalised token/gap structures) build the deeply nested multi-alternative '(a|b|...)' content model that cmplog cannot, producing a parent->c2 child and the false branch at valid.c:1125.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of 'if (cur == parent->c1)' which assigns parent->c2 = NULL.
The winner-only path executes the else branch parent->c2 = NULL and then re-enters via cur = parent->c2.
The freed node trees come from element-content allocation; the content-model node constructor is exercised far more by the loser, showing the loser builds shallow models.
value_profile_cmplog wins decisive pair 1 over cmplog at prob_div 0.80 with the value_profile delta.
In pair 1 the winner resolves 8/10 with hitcount 6 while the loser blocks 9 with hitcount 0.
value_profile_cmplog's feedback includes CMP_MAP gradient buckets in addition to edge counters.
grimoire wins decisive pair 2 over cmplog at prob_div 0.80 with the grimoire_structural delta.
In pair 2 the winner resolves 8/10 with hitcount 63 while the loser blocks 9 with hitcount 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=63  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage and Grimoire structural mutators on top of the cmplog stack, recombining generalised token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures

evidence_trail.claim: The branch sits in the DTD element-declaration parse chain, reached through xmlParseElementMixedContentDecl which builds OR/SEQ content trees.
The XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_SEQ and XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_OR multi-child content types are reached far more by the resolving runs at the free walk switch.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose <!ELEMENT> content model is a single-child (no SEQ/OR group, only c1) tree in which value_profile_cmplog and grimoire still resolve the false branch (parent->c2 = NULL) at the same rate as cmplog, showing the multi-child content-model feature is not what drives the divergence.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed bytes only directly show the loser's DTD prefix; the winner-resolving seed bytes do not literally display a multi-alternative '(a|b|...)' group, so the c2-sibling requirement is inferred from the source/coverage overlay rather than seen verbatim in winner bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural mutators build multi-declaration DTD shapes that cause xmlAddElementDecl to return NULL, triggering the null-elem true branch; cmplog generates only valid single-declaration DTDs so NULL is never returned.
signature.one_line: Grimoire assembles duplicate DTD declarations forcing NULL return from xmlAddElementDecl

analysis.summary_one_line: A DTD whose element declaration makes xmlAddElementDecl return NULL so the caller passes elem==NULL into xmlValidateElementDecl, taking the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML+DTD where a <!ELEMENT ...> declaration drives xmlAddElementDecl to return NULL (e.g. a redeclared/duplicate or otherwise rejected element decl), so the SAX2 caller sets elem=NULL and then calls xmlValidateElementDecl with that NULL elem, exercising the true side of `if (elem == NULL) return(1)`.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire-discovered winning seeds build structurally complete DTD declarations (DOCTYPE + element-name token runs like 'atttttttttt', repeated 'dtds/127772.dtd' paths); the per-role overlay shows the winner takes the NULL-elem path 21 times (W T=21) at both the caller's `if (elem == NULL)` and the blocker line, reaching `ctxt->valid = 0` (line 801, [W]-only).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds parse as a single valid <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM ...> declaration whose element decl succeeds, so xmlAddElementDecl never returns NULL; the loser takes the false side 143 times (L T=0 F=143) and instead descends into the MIXED-content body and redefinition checks (line 4324 L T=60), never producing a NULL elem.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and its structural mutators (GrimoireExtension/RecursiveReplacement/StringReplacement) splice and recurse on those generalised DTD token structures, letting it assemble the multi-declaration / redeclaration shape that makes xmlAddElementDecl return NULL — a structural recombination capability cmplog (havoc + I2S only, both fuzzers share I2S) lacks.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true branch is `if (elem == NULL) return(1)` in xmlValidateElementDecl, taken only when elem is NULL.
The 1-hop caller sets elem to NULL when xmlAddElementDecl fails and then still calls xmlValidateElementDecl with it.
The winner reaches the NULL-elem caller path that the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=2   L 800  T=21 F=18  T=0 F=149  if (elem == NULL)

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker line the winner takes the true side 21 times while the loser never does (T=0 F=143).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4314  T=21 F=18  T=0 F=143  if (elem == NULL) return(1);  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser instead descends into the MIXED-content body with elem non-NULL.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4324  T=0 F=18  T=60 F=83  if (elem->etype == XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_MIXED) {

Loser seeds parse as a single valid DOCTYPE/SYSTEM declaration with a one-character element name.
The winning fuzzer is grimoire and its delta over cmplog is grimoire's structural generalization + structural mutators (both already have I2S).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only way to take the true branch is a duplicate/rejected element declaration, in which cmplog reaches the NULL-elem branch as often as grimoire — showing the structural-recombination capability is unnecessary and plain I2S/havoc suffices.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I infer that elem becomes NULL specifically because xmlAddElementDecl rejects a duplicate/redeclared element, but the prompt only shows elem==NULL is reached, not the precise xmlAddElementDecl failure cause.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's structural mutators recombine generalized DTD/attribute token structures to produce multiple attribute declarations on one element including a prior xmlns entry; cmplog's I2S byte substitution cannot synthesize this multi-attribute nesting.
signature.one_line: Grimoire assembles multi-attribute DTD with prior xmlns entry cmplog cannot build

analysis.summary_one_line: A DTD ATTLIST declaring multiple attributes on one element where an earlier-declared attribute is named xmlns, so the loop scans an existing xmlns entry.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed external-subset DTD that declares an element with at least two attributes, where the first attribute already inserted into elemDef->attributes is named (or prefixed) "xmlns", so that when a second ATTLIST is added the while-loop at line 2142 finds a non-NULL tmp whose name equals BAD_CAST "xmlns" and takes the true side of line 2143. This requires deep, repeatedly-valid structural nesting: <!DOCTYPE> with SYSTEM dtd, an element, and multiple attribute declarations sharing an element, one being an xmlns namespace declaration.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds preserve the full valid skeleton (UTF8 header, <?l ?>, <!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">, <a>, nested <b k:...> children) while recombining generalized token/gap structures, producing inputs with multiple namespaced attribute declarations on one element. The branch reaches hitcount 76 and resolves in all 10 grimoire trials, and grimoire-discovered seeds carry GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireExtensionMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator ops that splice these structural fragments.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds keep a flat, shallow layout (a binary length prefix 06 00 00 00, then 127772.xml, then a single <?xml ...?><!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM ...> body) and are byte/word havoc + I2S mutations. They reach the blocker far less (hitcount 2, blocked in 8/10 trials) and on line 2143 take F=47 vs T=6, never building the second-attribute-with-prior-xmlns structure that drives the true side; I2S substitution alone cannot synthesize the multi-attribute nesting.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts the valid DTD/XML byte runs into <GAP>-templated tokens, and the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator/ExtensionMutator) recombine those tokens to emit DTDs with multiple attribute declarations including an xmlns one on a shared element. Plain cmplog (havoc + I2S only) cannot perform grammar-free structural recombination, so it rarely constructs the prior-xmlns-entry list that the line-2143 loop scans.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair at this branch is grimoire beating cmplog with the single-technique delta grimoire_structural.
Grimoire resolves the blocker in all 10 trials while cmplog is blocked in 8 of 10.
The winner reaches the branch far more often than the loser (hitcount 76 vs 2).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=76  loser=2

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the loop test comparing an existing attribute's name to the literal xmlns.
The branch is inside the loop that scans elemDef->attributes for prior xmlns entries before inserting a new attribute declaration.
Grimoire winner seeds preserve a deep valid DTD skeleton with element and nested namespaced children.
Winner seeds carry Grimoire structural mutation ops that recombine generalized fragments.
A winner seed shows nested namespaced child attributes such as <b k:...>.
Cmplog loser seeds are flat havoc/byte mutations with a leading binary length prefix, not structural recombination.
Loser seeds are produced by byte-level havoc mutators, not grammar-aware structural recombination.
At the blocker line the loser overwhelmingly takes the false side while the winner takes the true side relatively more often.
Grimoire builds on the full cmplog stack and adds the GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators, making the single-technique delta the structural recombination, not I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
it includes the `CmpLogObserver`, the `TracingStage`, and the
`I2SRandReplace` (i2s) stage — and ADDS a `GeneralizationStage` plus
Grimoire structural mutators

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire mutators splice and recurse on generalised token/gap structures (string-based, grammar-free structural mutation).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where a flat input with a single attribute (or a non-xmlns attribute name) drives the true side of line 2143 as often as the deeply-nested multi-attribute xmlns input, showing structural recombination is not required for the loop to find a prior xmlns entry.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from grimoire structural recombination specifically to constructing a prior xmlns attribute entry (vs grimoire merely producing more/deeper valid DTDs that reach the loop) is inferred from seed bytes and op names rather than directly traced.
